stories.title: Andy Burnham Set to Become UK's Next Prime Minister After Starmer Resignation
stories.summary: Following Keir Starmer's resignation as Labour leader, Andy Burnham has secured the backing of 322 Labour MPs to become the next Prime Minister. Starmer is expected to face his final PMQs on Wednesday before Burnham takes over at No. 10.

stories.title: Burnham Weighs Bigger Budget as Experts Lobby on Economic Policy
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is considering unveiling a bolstered budget later this year as allies and economic experts seek to persuade the incoming PM to pursue a more expansionary fiscal approach. Key adviser Miatta Fahnbulleh says people 'at the bottom' will have 'more money in their pockets'.

stories.title: Reeves Tells Burnham to Expect 'Shocks and Challenges' From Outset at No. 10
stories.summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves has urged Andy Burnham to arrive in Downing Street with a 'worked through plan', warning the incoming Prime Minister will be tested quickly by both economic pressures and international challenges.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, Legislation & Debates

stories.title: Starmer to Face Final PMQs on Wednesday; Immigration and Asylum Bill Debated
stories.summary: Parliament returns this week (13–17 July) with Sir Keir Starmer facing his final Prime Minister's Questions on Wednesday. MPs will also debate the Government's Immigration and Asylum Bill and consider amendments to the Representation of the People Bill.

stories.title: Hillsborough Law Bill Expected to Be Approved by MPs
stories.summary: The long-delayed bill to create a Hillsborough Law is expected to be approved by MPs after the government confirmed it could return to Parliament. The bill follows the 1989 FA Cup semi-final crush that left 97 fans dead.

stories.title: National Security (State Threats) Bill Debated
stories.summary: Parliament is scrutinising the National Security (State Threats) Bill, which would give the Home Secretary new powers to designate organisations as state threats — modelled on existing terrorist proscription powers.

stories.title: 322 Labour MPs Back Andy Burnham as Starmer's Successor
stories.summary: Burnham has secured overwhelming parliamentary backing in the Labour leadership contest following Starmer's resignation. His platform includes addressing living costs and a potential expanded budget later in 2026.

stories.title: GMB Warns Labour MPs That Donation Cap Could Endanger Party Funding
stories.summary: The GMB union has cautioned Labour MPs that a proposed cap on political donations could significantly harm the party's own financial position, warning of risks to the Labour–union funding relationship.

stories.title: UK Must Cap Political Donations to Stop the Rich Buying Influence
stories.summary: Opinion piece argues that whether Starmer or his successor acts, the UK should restrict the power of mega-donors to buy political influence, with Burnham seen as having a mandate to push through reform.

stories.title: Tory Centrists Defy Badenoch's Red Lines on Net Zero and ECHR
stories.summary: A Conservative pressure group calling for a 'broad church' party has pushed back against Kemi Badenoch's decision to exclude candidates who do not align with her positions on net zero and the European Convention on Human Rights.

stories.title: Cabinet Ministers Uncertain About Their Futures Under Burnham
stories.summary: Existing Cabinet ministers — many of whom served under Starmer — are said to have 'no clue' whether they will be retained or dismissed by incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham, creating significant uncertainty at the top of government.

stories.title: Ed Davey Calls to Defend UK Democracy After Tommy Robinson's Musk-Funded Russia Trip
stories.summary: Lib Dem leader Ed Davey voiced serious concern about the Musk family foundation funding Tommy Robinson's visit to Moscow, calling for a robust defence of UK democratic values against far-right and foreign influence.

stories.title: Lib Dems Push Burnham for Detail on Social Care and SEND Plans
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats have called on Andy Burnham, the incoming PM, to provide concrete commitments on fixing social care, tackling the SEND crisis, and supporting small businesses before taking office.

stories.title: Reform UK Would Lose 85% of Funding Under Proposed £100k Donation Cap
stories.summary: Analysis of Electoral Commission data shows Reform UK would have raised just £4.1m instead of £26.7m last year under a proposed £100,000 donation cap, potentially crippling the party's finances.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe Murdered — Police Arrest 28-Year-Old Suspect
stories.summary: Ann Widdecombe, former Conservative MP and Reform UK figure, has been killed in what police are treating as a murder investigation. A 28-year-old has been arrested and police say there is 'nothing to suggest' the killing was politically motivated.

stories.title: Farage Accused of Using Ann Widdecombe's Death as Political Propaganda
stories.summary: Nigel Farage faced criticism from a close friend of the former MP for speculating publicly on a live police investigation into Widdecombe's death and using the tragedy for political ends.

stories.title: Peter Murrell at Centre of Fresh £1.5m Missing Cash Scandal
stories.summary: Disgraced former SNP chief executive Peter Murrell is reported to have had 'tight control' of a Scottish independence campaign group whose finances are now under police investigation for an alleged £1.5m discrepancy.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government Reshuffles, Senior Appointments & Cross-Department Policy

stories.title: Defence Secretary John Healey Resigns Over Defence Spending Plans
stories.summary: Defence Secretary John Healey has resigned from Cabinet, saying he cannot support the government's current defence investment plans. His departure, along with that of Minister Al Carns, marks significant turbulence in the outgoing Starmer Cabinet.

stories.title: Home Office to Spend £250m on Protecting Jewish Communities
stories.summary: A three-year government plan will deliver an extra 500 officers to patrol Jewish neighbourhoods across England and Wales following a series of antisemitic incidents.

stories.title: Green MP Hannah Spencer Wins Byelection, Plans Maximum Workplace Temperature Bill
stories.summary: Green MP Hannah Spencer, fresh from winning a byelection, plans to introduce a private member's bill to legislate a maximum workplace temperature, citing summer heatwaves causing 'absolute chaos' for workers.

stories.title: Lords Active This Week: Immigration, Representation of the People Bill Amendments
stories.summary: The House of Lords is sitting this week to consider amendments to major government bills, including the Representation of the People Bill and the Immigration and Asylum Bill, as Parliament presses ahead with an ambitious legislative agenda.

stories.title: UK Ratifies High Seas Treaty — Lords Debate Marine Biodiversity Protections
stories.summary: The UK ratified the Biodiversity Beyond National Jurisdiction (BBNJ) treaty on 10 July 2026, establishing a UK legal framework to protect marine areas beyond national jurisdiction. The House of Lords debated the landmark legislation.
